title: Physical Device and Systems Inventory
description: Physical devices and systems within the organisation are inventoried.
purpose: Ensures all physical devices are known and managed as part of the overall cybersecurity programme.
implementationGuidance: Maintain a comprehensive, current inventory of all physical devices and systems. Include hardware type, model, serial number, location, owner, and network connection. Update the inventory when devices are added, removed, or changed.
bestPractices: Automate asset discovery using network scanning tools
Update inventory within 24 hours of asset changes
Reconcile inventory against physical assets annually

title: Software Platforms and Applications Inventory
description: Software platforms and applications within the organisation are inventoried.
purpose: Ensures all software is known, authorised, and managed.
implementationGuidance: Maintain a software asset inventory covering all applications, platforms, operating systems, and licences. Identify unauthorised software. Implement application allowlisting on critical systems.
bestPractices: Use SAM (Software Asset Management) tools for automated software discovery
Implement application allowlisting on servers
Reconcile software inventory quarterly

title: Organisational Communication and Data Flows
description: Organisational communication and data flows are mapped.
purpose: Provides visibility into how data moves through the organisation to identify risks.
implementationGuidance: Create and maintain network and data flow diagrams. Identify all external connections. Document data flows for sensitive data. Update diagrams when the environment changes.
evidenceExamples: Data flow maps for sensitive data
bestPractices: Maintain data flow diagrams as living documents
Review and validate diagrams semi-annually
Use automated network mapping tools to verify accuracy

title: Resources Prioritised Based on Classification and Criticality
description: Resources (e.g., hardware, devices, data, time, personnel, and software) are prioritised based on their classification, criticality, and business value.
purpose: Enables risk-based prioritisation of cybersecurity protections.
implementationGuidance: Classify and prioritise all assets by business criticality and data sensitivity. Use criticality ratings to guide security investment and incident response prioritisation. Review classifications when business priorities change.
bestPractices: Align asset classification with business impact analysis
Review criticality ratings annually
Use criticality to drive incident response prioritisation

title: Organisation's Role in Supply Chain
description: The organisation's role in the supply chain is identified and communicated.
purpose: Ensures the organisation understands its supply chain position to manage associated cybersecurity risks.
implementationGuidance: Document your organisation's role in all relevant supply chains. Identify dependencies on external organisations. Communicate your security requirements to supply chain partners. Understand cybersecurity obligations from upstream customers.
bestPractices: Map your full supply chain including sub-suppliers
Communicate cybersecurity requirements to all partners
Monitor supply chain for security incidents

title: Information Security Policy
description: Organisational information security policy is established and communicated.
purpose: Establishes the governance framework for the organisation's cybersecurity programme.
implementationGuidance: Develop and publish an information security policy approved by leadership. Communicate to all personnel. Review at least annually. Align with applicable regulatory requirements.
evidenceExamples: Approved and published information security policy
bestPractices: Obtain board or executive approval for the information security policy
Review and update annually or after significant changes
Align policy with regulatory requirements applicable to your industry

title: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
description: Information security roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with internal roles and external partners.
purpose: Ensures clear accountability for cybersecurity activities.
implementationGuidance: Define and document cybersecurity roles and responsibilities. Assign security responsibilities to specific individuals. Coordinate with external partners on shared security responsibilities. Review annually.
bestPractices: Define clear security responsibilities in job descriptions
Conduct annual review of security role assignments
Document responsibilities for shared functions with partners

title: Asset Vulnerabilities Identified and Documented
description: Asset vulnerabilities are identified and documented.
purpose: Provides a current view of vulnerabilities across all organisational assets.
implementationGuidance: Conduct regular vulnerability scans on all assets. Maintain a vulnerability tracking register. Document vulnerability severity, affected systems, and remediation status. Monitor threat intelligence for new vulnerabilities.
bestPractices: Scan all assets at least monthly
Monitor NVD and vendor advisories for new vulnerabilities
Track all vulnerabilities from identification to closure

title: Threats Identified and Documented
description: Threats, both internal and external, are identified and documented.
purpose: Enables proactive threat management based on a current threat landscape.
implementationGuidance: Conduct threat identification as part of the risk assessment process. Use threat intelligence to identify relevant threats. Document internal and external threats. Update threat profiles when the threat landscape changes.
evidenceExamples: Threat register with internal and external threats
bestPractices: Subscribe to sector-specific threat intelligence (ISACs)
Update threat profiles quarterly
Include threat intelligence in executive security briefings

title: Threats, Vulnerabilities, Likelihoods and Impacts Used to Determine Risk
description: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine risk.
purpose: Enables risk-based prioritisation of cybersecurity activities.
implementationGuidance: Use a consistent risk calculation methodology combining threat likelihood and impact to determine risk ratings. Document risk ratings for all identified risks. Use risk ratings to prioritise remediation.
requiredDocuments: Risk assessment records with likelihood and impact
evidenceExamples: Risk register with likelihood × impact ratings
bestPractices: Use qualitative or quantitative risk ratings consistently
Review risk ratings quarterly
Use risk ratings to drive budget and investment decisions

title: Identities and Credentials Managed
category: Protect: Identity Management and Access Control (PR.AC)
description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorised devices, users, and processes.
purpose: Ensures identity and credential lifecycle is managed to prevent unauthorised access.
implementationGuidance: Implement identity lifecycle management covering provisioning, management, review, and deprovisioning. Enforce unique credentials. Implement credential policies (length, complexity, expiry). Conduct regular access reviews.
bestPractices: Implement IAM solution for centralised identity management
Conduct quarterly access reviews for all privileged accounts
Automate deprovisioning upon termination

title: Remote Access Managed
category: Protect: Identity Management and Access Control (PR.AC)
description: Remote access is managed.
purpose: Ensures remote access to organisational systems is controlled and secure.
implementationGuidance: Implement a VPN or zero-trust remote access solution. Require MFA for all remote access. Log and monitor remote sessions. Define and enforce remote access policies.
bestPractices: Require MFA for all remote access
Implement zero-trust network access for higher security
Monitor remote access sessions for anomalous activity

title: Access Permissions and Authorisations Managed
category: Protect: Identity Management and Access Control (PR.AC)
description: Access permissions and authorisations are managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.
purpose: Ensures access is granted based on need, incorporating least privilege and SOD.
implementationGuidance: Implement RBAC with least privilege. Enforce SOD for critical functions. Review access permissions regularly. Remove excessive permissions. Require approval for all access grants.
bestPractices: Review all access permissions quarterly
Implement just-in-time access for privileged operations
Automate SOD conflict detection

title: All Users Informed and Trained
description: All users are informed and trained.
purpose: Ensures all personnel have the cybersecurity knowledge needed to protect the organisation.
implementationGuidance: Implement annual security awareness training for all personnel. Cover phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, incident reporting, and applicable policies. Track completion. Provide role-specific training for security personnel.
evidenceExamples: Annual training completion evidence for all staff
Training content covering key security topics
bestPractices: Train all personnel annually with quarterly refreshers
Use multiple training formats (video, interactive, in-person)
Measure training effectiveness through assessments

title: Data-at-Rest Protected
description: Data-at-rest is protected.
purpose: Ensures sensitive data cannot be read if storage media is compromised.
implementationGuidance: Implement encryption for all sensitive data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent. Apply to databases, file systems, endpoints, and backup media. Manage encryption keys securely.
bestPractices: Apply AES-256 to all sensitive data at rest
Use dedicated KMS for encryption key management
Verify encryption coverage through periodic audits

title: Data-in-Transit Protected
description: Data-in-transit is protected.
purpose: Prevents sensitive data from being intercepted during transmission.
implementationGuidance: Implement TLS 1.2 or higher for all sensitive data in transit. Prohibit transmission of sensitive data over unencrypted channels. Implement VPN for site-to-site communications.
bestPractices: Require TLS 1.2+ for all web and API communications
Implement certificate pinning for mobile applications
Block all legacy protocols (SSL, TLS 1.0/1.1)

title: Baseline Configuration Established
category: Protect: Information Protection Processes and Procedures (PR.IP)
description: A baseline configuration of information technology/industrial control systems is created and maintained incorporating security principles.
purpose: Establishes a known, secure starting point for all systems.
implementationGuidance: Define security configuration baselines for all system types. Apply CIS Benchmarks or equivalent. Enforce baselines through configuration management tools. Monitor for deviations.
bestPractices: Automate baseline enforcement with configuration management tools
Scan for deviations weekly
Update baselines only through formal change management

title: Backups Conducted, Maintained, and Tested
category: Protect: Information Protection Processes and Procedures (PR.IP)
description: Backups of information are conducted, maintained, and tested.
purpose: Ensures data can be recovered following loss, corruption, or ransomware attack.
implementationGuidance: Implement automated backups for all critical systems and data. Follow 3-2-1 rule. Store backups offline or in immutable storage to protect against ransomware. Test restoration quarterly.
bestPractices: Store backups in immutable storage to protect against ransomware
Test restoration quarterly at minimum
Define and verify RTO/RPO achievement in recovery tests

title: Vulnerability Management Plan
category: Protect: Information Protection Processes and Procedures (PR.IP)
description: A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented.
purpose: Provides a structured approach to identifying and remediating vulnerabilities.
implementationGuidance: Develop a vulnerability management plan covering scanning frequency, assessment methodology, prioritisation approach, remediation SLAs, and exception handling. Implement the plan and measure outcomes.
bestPractices: Define SLAs: critical 24h, high 7 days, medium 30 days, low 90 days
Track mean time to remediate as a KPI
Review vulnerability management plan annually

title: Remote Maintenance Approved, Logged, and Monitored
description: Remote maintenance of organisational assets is approved, logged, and performed in a manner that prevents unauthorised access.
purpose: Ensures remote maintenance activities are controlled and audited.
implementationGuidance: Require management approval for all remote maintenance. Use encrypted connections for remote maintenance. Log all remote maintenance sessions. Monitor sessions in real time where feasible. Remove access immediately after maintenance.
bestPractices: Use PAM tools to manage and record remote maintenance sessions
Require dual authorisation for critical system maintenance
Review remote maintenance logs weekly

title: Audit/Log Records Determined, Documented, Implemented and Reviewed
description: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed in accordance with policy.
purpose: Provides a comprehensive audit trail for security investigations and compliance.
implementationGuidance: Define logging requirements for all systems. Implement centralised log management. Review logs regularly. Protect logs from modification. Retain logs per policy.
bestPractices: Centralise all logs in SIEM for correlation
Review critical system logs daily
Implement automated alerting for security events

title: Network Operations Baseline Established
description: A baseline of network operations and expected data flows for users and systems is established and managed.
purpose: Enables detection of anomalous network activity by comparison to a known baseline.
implementationGuidance: Profile normal network behaviour (traffic volumes, flow patterns, user access patterns). Document the baseline. Monitor for deviations. Update baseline as normal operations evolve.
bestPractices: Use UEBA tools to automate baseline profiling and anomaly detection
Review network baselines quarterly
Alert on significant deviations from baseline

title: Network Monitored to Detect Potential Cybersecurity Events
description: The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events.
purpose: Enables real-time detection of attacks and security anomalies on the network.
implementationGuidance: Implement IDS/IPS at network boundaries and key internal segments. Deploy SIEM for centralised monitoring. Define alerting rules. Conduct 24/7 monitoring of critical networks.
bestPractices: Implement NIDS and HIDS for comprehensive coverage
Monitor east-west traffic within the network in addition to perimeter
Conduct threat hunting to supplement automated monitoring

title: Monitoring for Unauthorised Personnel, Connections, Devices, and Software
description: Monitoring for unauthorised personnel, connections, devices, and software is performed.
purpose: Detects unauthorised elements in the environment before they can cause harm.
implementationGuidance: Implement continuous monitoring for unauthorised devices (NAC), users (IAM monitoring), connections (firewall and IDS), and software (application control/allowlisting). Alert on detection. Investigate and remove promptly.
bestPractices: Implement NAC to detect and quarantine unauthorised devices
Use UEBA to detect anomalous user activity
Alert immediately on unauthorised software detected on endpoints

title: Event Detection Information Communicated to Appropriate Parties
description: Event detection information is communicated.
purpose: Ensures detected security events are communicated to the appropriate people for response.
implementationGuidance: Define communication procedures for security events. Establish escalation paths from detection tools to security operations and management. Test communication procedures. Define SLAs for event notification.
bestPractices: Implement automated alerting through multiple channels (email, SMS, pager)
Define escalation paths for different severity levels
Test communication procedures quarterly

title: Response Plan Executed
description: Response plan is executed during or after an incident.
purpose: Ensures the organisation has and uses a structured response plan for security incidents.
implementationGuidance: Develop a comprehensive incident response plan. Test the plan at least annually. Ensure all IRT members know their roles. Execute the plan for all incidents. Document response actions.
bestPractices: Test with tabletop and live exercises annually
Keep the IRP current with personnel and system changes
Measure plan effectiveness through incident metrics

title: Incidents Reported Consistent with Established Criteria
description: Incidents are reported consistent with established criteria.
purpose: Ensures incidents are reported to the right people at the right time.
implementationGuidance: Define incident classification and reporting criteria. Identify all internal and external reporting requirements. Define reporting timelines for each incident type. Train IRT on reporting obligations.
bestPractices: Define reporting criteria for each incident type and severity
Automate internal notifications through SIEM and ITSM integration
Maintain a regulatory reporting calendar with deadline tracking

title: Notifications from Detection Systems Investigated
description: Notifications from detection systems are investigated.
purpose: Ensures all security alerts are investigated promptly and effectively.
implementationGuidance: Define procedures for investigating security alerts. Triage alerts by severity. Define investigation SLAs. Document investigation findings. Escalate based on findings. Close investigations with documented conclusions.
bestPractices: Triage all alerts within 15 minutes
Investigate all high and critical alerts within 1 hour
Document all investigation findings for post-incident learning

title: Incidents Contained
description: Incidents are contained.
purpose: Limits the impact of security incidents through rapid containment actions.
implementationGuidance: Define containment strategies for different incident types (network isolation, account disabling, system quarantine). Implement technical capabilities for rapid containment. Test containment procedures. Document containment actions.
bestPractices: Define containment actions for each major incident type in advance
Implement automated containment capabilities (SOAR)
Measure mean time to contain (MTTC) as a key IR metric

title: Recovery Plan Executed
description: Recovery plan is executed during or after a cybersecurity incident.
purpose: Ensures the organisation can recover to normal operations following a security incident.
implementationGuidance: Develop a recovery plan including procedures for restoring systems, data, and operations after an incident. Test the plan annually. Document recovery procedures for each major system. Define recovery priorities.
bestPractices: Test recovery plan at least annually
Define RTO for all critical systems
Restore from known-good backups after ransomware incidents

title: Recovery Plans Incorporate Lessons Learned
description: Recovery plans incorporate lessons learned.
purpose: Improves future recovery and response through learning from past incidents.
implementationGuidance: Conduct post-incident reviews for all significant incidents. Document lessons learned. Update recovery plans based on findings. Share lessons across the organisation where appropriate.
requiredDocuments: Plan update procedures from lessons learned
evidenceExamples: Recovery plan updates from lessons learned
bestPractices: Conduct post-incident reviews within 5 business days
Share lessons learned organisation-wide
Track plan improvements driven by lessons learned

title: Recovery Activities Communicated to Stakeholders
description: Recovery activities are communicated to internal and external stakeholders as well as executive and management teams.
purpose: Ensures stakeholders are informed throughout the recovery process.
implementationGuidance: Define communication procedures for recovery activities. Identify all stakeholders who need recovery status updates. Define update frequency. Prepare communication templates. Assign a communications lead for incidents.
bestPractices: Provide recovery status updates at defined intervals (e.g., every 4 hours for major incidents)
Assign a dedicated communications lead for incident recovery
Pre-draft recovery communication templates for common incident types
